title: carduus crispus subsp. agrestis
intro: Carduus crispus subsp. agrestis, also known as the wild cardoon or prickly thistle, is a subspecies of the cardoon plant, characterized by its distinctive prickly stems and leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Carduus crispus subsp. agrestis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
